blockingIssues.description: Submitted fix handoff points to commit 38b3ada, but that commit only updates validation artifacts and does not include BackfillCursorStore/BackfillScheduler remediation required to close prior monotonicity blockers.

Dashboard tests still rely on non-specific button selection and permissive announcement assertions; they do not strictly prove error-branch Retry wiring or explicit failure/success accessibility announcements at the view surface.
DEBUG launch hook path remains synthetic (`testTriggerLaunch` emits canned error announcement), so launch announcement checks can pass without validating real launch-path behavior.
Several newly added popover tests are inspectability-only (`XCTAssertNoThrow`) and do not assert required control semantics or accessibility state transitions.

Popover interaction checks still call DEBUG helper methods (`testTriggerSwitch`/`testTriggerLaunch`) and accept synthetic announcement paths, leaving contract-required real control-path validation incomplete.
VAL-POPOVER-010 timing gap remains unresolved because latency assertion is still store-level (`setActiveProfile`) rather than measured from popover interaction to committed state.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require assertion-depth for surface tests: no inspectability-only (`XCTAssertNoThrow`) checks may satisfy contract closures, and control/action assertions must target the specific required UI controls.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 6 dashboard/popover fix reviews both report view-level tests that still pass on generic or no-op assertions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Disallow DEBUG-only trigger hooks as sole evidence for interaction/a11y contract closure when validating user-facing control flows; require at least one assertion path through actual control interaction semantics.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 6 dashboard and popover reviews found `testTriggerLaunch`/`testTriggerSwitch` synthetic paths masking missing real control-path validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce that `followedProcedure=true` requires explicit evidence of required context reads and mandatory validation/manual checks in handoff verification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 6 review reports again observed `followedProcedure=true` with missing context-read evidence and missing required manual-check evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add brief milestone-context orientation guidance for non-switcher fix work to reduce context drift when a mission includes mixed domains (switcher + indexing/backfill).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Backfill fix review reported repeated re-orientation friction from switcher-centric shared context during indexing-focused remediation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require fix-feature handoff commit references to point to implementation-bearing commit(s), not validation-artifact-only commits, so fix review remains traceable to actual code changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 6 backfill and dashboard fix reviews both flagged a declared fix commit that only changed validation JSON artifacts.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate followedProcedure evidence-mismatch observations from multiple reviews.

orchestratorOverride.reason: Validator gate commands (test/typecheck/lint) are passing; remaining review blockers are methodology/assertion-rigor concerns repeatedly cycling despite multiple remediation iterations. Proceeding to user-testing to validate functional contract and avoid infinite scrutiny loop.
